calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-18-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George Sullivan for Ronald Charles (Administrator). Cynthia Lee for and with Stephanie Jackson. Barbara Crane-Rooks in Propria Persona.

Ms. Lee stated that she had no evidence that Essie Crane Jr. was the biological Son of the Decedent, that she was not sure of the birthright of Stephanie and that she had not found any paper work regarding an adoption. In addition, she stated that the Decedent always thought of Essie as his Son, that he communicated this to the community and the church and that Stephanie and Barbara (Sisters) have always known the Decedent as their Grandfather. Mr. Sullivan argued that the parties had plenty of time to do research and that they had no standing. The Court questioned the inventory and expressed concern that there was no personal property or bank accounts. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the personal property and bank accounts. The Court stated that it was a legal decision based on proof that exist or doesn't exist and that evidence that supports the law is what would determine the decision. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-19-2009 Continued To Jun-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: George Sullivan for Ronald Charles (Petitioner). Cynthia Lee for and with Stephanie Jackson.

The Court stated that Ronald Charles (Grandson) was entitled to act. Ms. Lee stipulated to Mr. Charles being appointed, requested special notice for the Petition To Establish Heirship, stated that her Client believed that there were other assets, but that she was not able to confirm this as her Client was not allowed to enter the home. Mr. Sullivan stated that the property was the only asset in the estate and that he would like to get the property sold. The Court appointed Ronald Charles as the Adminstrator, (signing the order for the same in open court). The bond was set at $10,000.00 and will be increased if other assets are found. The Court directed Mr. Sullivan to set up a date for everyone to be allowed to go to the home.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Brown.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-29-2008 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
